rationale: The answer notices the repeated downstream overlay pattern and moves partly upstream by naming the structural problem: requirements are scattered and students only fix the previous failure. It also proposes consolidation into one submission checklist and stopping future scattered reminders. However, it does not refuse the next overlay as the default; it starts with “可以加” and supplies the exact new reminder copy for the submission page. That observes the fail signal of adding another explanation/reminder layer, though with a partial brake and consolidation warning.
